title: Buttery Vietnamese Sirloin Steak Stir-fry
description: A dense and sick-day friendly dish featuring halal-friendly sirloin steak stir-fried with a creamy sauce in a quick, easy-to-cook meal.

ingredients.name: 1 lb (450g) halal sirloin steak
ingredients.notes: ensure it's halal-certified and from a trusted source

ingredients.name: 1/2 cup creamy peanut sauce (store-bought or homemade)
1 tsp fish sauce (if needed for taste adjustment)

instructions: Preheat your oven to 425°F (220°C). Season the sirloin steak with salt and pepper on both sides.
Prepare the stir-fry: In a small bowl, mix the creamy peanut sauce. Set aside.
In a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat, add butter and once melted, add chopped onions. Cook until they turn translucent and slightly golden.
Add minced garlic to the skillet and cook for about 1 minute until fragrant. Mix in the stir-fry sauce with the onions and garlic. Let it simmer for a few minutes to let the flavors meld together.

Place the sirloin steak on top of the onion mixture, ensuring both sides are coated evenly with the peanut sauce. Cook for 2-3 minutes per side or until the meat is cooked to your desired doneness (internal temperature should reach at least 145°F/65°C).
Once fully cooked, remove the steak from the skillet and let it rest for a few minutes before slicing into thin strips.
Garnish with fresh cilantro and serve hot over steamed rice or noodles.
